summary: Iteration 51 — Groups A+C features (enriched 401 gate, first-share modal, bio field, tokenized RSS feed) verified end-to-end. Backend: 19/19 new pytest cases in test_iteration_51.py pass; previous iteration_50/49/public_library suites still 41/41 green (60 total). Frontend Playwright covered all 7 specified flows: (1) enriched anon gate with avatar+book-count+top-fandom+bio for opted-in handle, (2) fallback heading for nonexistent handle (gate-preview absent), (3) bio textarea save + persistence, (4) bio 280-char hard cap on client, (5) bio surfacing on /u/<h> cover and /u/<h>/library owner block, (6) RSS panel appears with ?token= URL after public-library toggle ON, (7) RSS regenerate produces a new URL, (8) first-share-modal flow incl. FB/Twitter hrefs, copy/close btns, idempotency on second toggle, (9) no-handle guard fires toast and prevents toggle flip / modal.
critical_code_review_comments: friend_library.library_preview correctly mirrors the 404 invariant (not-opted-in == nonexistent), AV-infected books excluded from total, and approval_status gated to (None, 'approved') — consistent with the public_library + OG share endpoints.

library_rss_feed uses secrets.compare_digest(token, owner['rss_token']) for constant-time comparison — good defense against timing attacks. 404 returned uniformly for missing token, wrong token, not-opted-in, and nonexistent handle; bodies confirmed byte-identical in test_404_bodies_identical_no_enumeration.

first_public_share_shown_at is stamped once via the same $set on the visibility update — atomic with the visibility flip, no race. set_public_library_visibility correctly reads the prior doc before computing first_time so concurrent toggles won't double-trigger.
BioBody uses Pydantic Field(max_length=280) so server returns 422 on >280; frontend hard-caps at 280 via slice (verified DOM length == 280 after fill of 300). Defense-in-depth matches spec.

RSS XML uses a hand-rolled escaper. It covers &,<,>,",'  which is sufficient for RSS 2.0 element content. Consider switching to xml.sax.saxutils.escape long-term but not a blocker — current output parses cleanly with ElementTree in test_anon_with_correct_token_returns_valid_xml.
test_credentials: Opted-in fixture: TEST_fe_opted_e24c9d@example.com / hunter2pw! / @febopta508d (verified). First-share modal fresh user created during run: TEST_fs_3ec55e15@example.com / @fs6a8b15 (already-toggled). No-handle user: TEST_nohandle_37be5b4a@example.com (no @handle).
seed_data_creation: Three fresh users registered during this run via /api/auth/register: one for first-share-modal happy path (handle claimed via PATCH /api/auth/username), one for no-handle guard (no handle ever set), plus an ephemeral one used inside TestFirstShareModalFlag.test_first_time_toggle_returns_modal_true_subsequent_false. Bio field on the opted fixture user was mutated to 'FE bio test zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz' (≤280 chars) and persists; clean to reset via PUT /api/account/bio {bio:''} if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Handle claim endpoint is PATCH /api/auth/username {username:...} (NOT /api/account/handle — 404s). Use that for any future fresh-user-with-handle fixtures. /api/users/{u}/library-preview is anon-allowed and intentionally leaks count + top fandom + bio + display name + avatar — that's the documented design for gate conversion, do not flag as a leak. /api/feeds/library/{u}.rss requires ?token= matching the user's rss_token; constant-time check + uniform 404 invariants are tested. first_public_share_shown_at is server-side stamped once — mongosh unset is the only way to retrigger the modal on the same user. Bio is hard-capped 280 chars both client (slice) and server (Pydantic max_length=422).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Implementation matches spec across all four features. Suggest no further fixes.
